title: Comedy TV
subtitle: Workplace ensembles, warm-affection comedies, dense writing. No cringe-driven discomfort.

sections.name: I. Already Loved
sections.desc: Your established comedy TV pillars. Listed for tracking and rewatch.

items.title: Schitt's Creek
items.pitch: Wealthy family loses everything and ends up in a small town they once bought as a joke. Warmth and dignity earned slowly across six seasons.

critics.quote: Won 9 Emmys in 2020 — the first comedy to sweep all four acting categories in a single year.

items.title: Arrested Development
items.seasons: Seasons 1-3 are the canonical run; 4-5 on Netflix considered weaker
items.pitch: Wealthy California family loses everything and the second-eldest son tries to keep them together. Densest joke construction in modern television.

critics.quote: Six Emmys for Season 1. Seasons 1-3 routinely cited among the greatest sitcoms ever made.

items.title: 30 Rock
items.pitch: Tina Fey runs a sketch show. Her network is run by Alec Baldwin's executive. The jokes-per-minute count is the highest in modern American sitcom history.

items.title: Parks and Recreation
items.pitch: Indiana Parks Department employee tries to build a park. The found-family ensemble is the heart.

critics.quote: Critical reputation grew steadily across its run. Now considered the warm-ensemble template.

items.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
items.pitch: NYPD precinct comedy with a deeply diverse ensemble. Schur's third great workplace comedy after The Office and Parks.

items.title: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia
items.seasons: Still going; consensus best stretch is roughly S2-S9
items.pitch: Five terrible people run a Philadelphia bar. Now one of the longest-running live-action sitcoms in American TV history.

critics.quote: Cult-then-mainstream. Dennis-as-character analysis has become its own discourse.

items.title: Studio 60 on the Sunset Strip
items.pitch: Sorkin's failed follow-up to West Wing. About a sketch comedy show. Critically dismissed at the time; reputation has slowly improved.

critics.quote: Polarizing on release; defenders have grown. Some consider the writing per scene as strong as anything Sorkin has done.

items.title: NewsRadio
items.pitch: AM news radio station ensemble. Phil Hartman, Dave Foley, Andy Dick, Maura Tierney. One of the great workplace ensembles.

items.title: Designing Women
items.pitch: Atlanta interior design firm run by four very different women. Politically pointed, sharply written, ensemble-first.

items.title: QI
items.seasons: Stephen Fry hosted A-M (Series 1-13); Sandi Toksvig from N onward
items.pitch: British panel show about quite interesting things. Educational, dryly funny, ensemble of recurring guests.

items.title: Top Gear (Clarkson era)
items.pitch: Three middle-aged Britons review cars and stage increasingly absurd challenges. The chemistry is the show.

critics.quote: Most-watched factual show in the world during peak years. Clarkson era ended after the BBC firing in 2015.

items.title: The Grand Tour
items.pitch: Top Gear's hosts continued on Amazon. Eventually moved to feature-length specials. Final special aired 2024.

critics.quote: Critical consensus that it found its footing once it transitioned from studio format to specials.

items.title: Tacoma FD
items.pitch: Broken Lizard's firehouse comedy. Tacoma firefighters have very little to do because it rains all the time.

items.title: The League
items.pitch: Fantasy football league friends being terrible to each other. Largely improvised. FX, not network.

items.title: Pushing Daisies
items.pitch: A pie-maker can bring the dead back to life with a touch. Procedural mystery wrapped in storybook visuals.

critics.quote: Won 7 Emmys despite short run. Cult devotion remains strong.

items.title: Silicon Valley
items.pitch: Mike Judge's tech-startup satire. The middle-out compression algorithm and the dick joke math sequence are TV history.

items.title: Alpha House
items.pitch: Garry Trudeau's Doonesbury-flavored comedy about four Republican senators sharing a DC house. John Goodman anchors.

items.title: Scrubs
items.seasons: S1-S8 are the canonical run; S9 (Med School) is widely disowned
items.pitch: Single-camera medical comedy with surrealist fantasy sequences. The emotional swerves were unprecedented for the format at the time.

items.title: Ted Lasso
items.pitch: American football coach takes over an English Premier League club. The kindness-as-strategy show became the COVID-era prestige comedy hit.

sections.name: III. High Priority — Sorkin & Sorkin-adjacent half-hour
sections.desc: Workplace ensemble, dense dialogue, idealistic protagonists. Direct fits given your Sorkin loves.

items.title: Sports Night
items.whyPriority: Sorkin's first TV show. Sports center-style cable news ensemble. The half-hour Sorkin you haven't seen yet — direct fit given West Wing/Newsroom loves.
items.pitch: Behind-the-scenes of a SportsCenter-like nightly show. Felicity Huffman, Peter Krause, Josh Charles. Sorkin's dialogue at its tightest because he had less time per episode.

critics.quote: Cancelled prematurely by ABC. Critically beloved; consistently ranked among the great single-season-or-two American comedies.

sections.name: IV. High Priority — Warm Ensemble Lane
sections.desc: The 30 Rock / Parks / Brooklyn 99 / Schitt's Creek family. Workplace or found-family, dense writing, warmth toward characters.

items.title: Community
items.seasons: S1-S3 are peak; S4 widely panned (Harmon fired); S5-S6 recovered. Movie announced.
items.whyPriority: Community college study group. Dan Harmon's structural mania makes it the cleverest workplace-adjacent ensemble in TV. Direct fit for Arrested Development loves.
items.pitch: Disgraced lawyer joins a community college Spanish study group. The characters become a found family. Genre-pastiche episodes (Western, paintball, claymation, multi-timeline) are TV-history-grade.

critics.quote: Cult-to-canonical reputation arc. The 'six seasons and a movie' rallying cry came true.

items.title: The Good Place
items.whyPriority: Schur's metaphysical comedy — same warm-ensemble DNA as Parks/Brooklyn 99 but applied to philosophy and the afterlife. The Season 1 finale twist is one of TV's best.
items.pitch: A morally questionable woman dies and ends up in heaven by mistake. She tries to become a good person before they figure out.

critics.quote: Critical reputation grew across run. Final season widely cited as one of the best comedy endings in years.

items.title: Detectorists
items.whyPriority: Two middle-aged English metal-detector hobbyists. Mackenzie Crook (The Office UK, Pirates of the Caribbean). Gentle, slow, beautifully written. Direct fit if Schitt's Creek warmth landed.
items.pitch: Two friends with metal detectors search English fields for treasure. The treasure is mostly junk. The friendship is the whole show.

critics.quote: Won BAFTAs across all three seasons. Routinely cited among the greatest British comedies of the 2010s.

items.title: Hacks
items.dir: Lucia Aniello, Paul W. Downs, Jen Statsky
items.whyPriority: Jean Smart as a legacy Vegas comedian paired with a young writer. Crackling dialogue, warm-ensemble core, sharp on the comedy industry. The Hacks/Schitt's Creek/Ted Lasso sweet-spot.
items.pitch: Aging Vegas comedy headliner is forced to work with a fired Gen-Z writer. Generational collision becomes mentor-protégé bond.

critics.quote: Won Outstanding Comedy at 2024 Emmys, beating The Bear. Smart has won three Emmys for the role.

items.title: Only Murders in the Building
items.whyPriority: Steve Martin, Martin Short, Selena Gomez. Three neighbors podcast about murders in their Upper West Side building. Each season is a self-contained mystery. Cozy + clever.
items.pitch: Three true-crime obsessed neighbors investigate murders in their Upper West Side building, one season at a time.

critics.quote: Multiple Emmy nominations. Considered the rare prestige-cozy fusion that works.

items.title: Documentary Now!
items.dir: Bill Hader, Fred Armisen, Seth Meyers
items.whyPriority: Hader/Armisen mockumentary parodies — each episode a precise pastiche of a real famous documentary. Rachel Dratch in the Stop Making Sense parody alone is worth the price of entry.
items.pitch: Each episode loving parodies a specific real documentary or genre. The Grey Gardens, Salesman, and Talking Heads parodies are the standouts.

critics.quote: Critical darling. Vulture and other outlets have ranked it among the best comedies of the 2010s.

items.title: What We Do in the Shadows (TV)
items.whyPriority: FX series of the 2014 film. Three vampires share a Staten Island house with a newer vampire and a familiar. Surprisingly warm given the premise. Ended in 2024, complete.
items.pitch: Three centuries-old vampires plus an energy vampire share a Staten Island house. Documentary crew watches.

critics.quote: Six straight Emmy nominations for Outstanding Comedy Series.

items.title: Veep
items.whyPriority: On your queue. Iannucci's American take after The Thick of It. Julia Louis-Dreyfus as VP-then-President. The profanity is poetry. Ended cleanly.
items.pitch: Selina Meyer's office in pursuit of power. Imagine The West Wing with no idealism and 10x the swearing.

critics.quote: Louis-Dreyfus won six consecutive Lead Actress Emmys for the role. Show won three Outstanding Comedy Series.

items.title: Better Off Ted
items.whyPriority: Cult corporate comedy. R&D lab at a soulless mega-corp. Portia de Rossi is brilliant. Cancelled too soon.
items.pitch: Veridian Dynamics will weaponize anything. Ted has to manage the lab.

critics.quote: Critical favorite. Cancelled despite strong reviews; cult reputation has only grown.

items.title: Damned
items.whyPriority: You requested. Jo Brand and Alan Davies in a UK Children's Services office. Bleakly funny, warm at the core, in the QI sensibility.
items.pitch: Social workers cope with bureaucracy and impossible cases. Black-comedy texture with deep affection for the characters.

items.title: The Bear
items.whyPriority: Categorized as comedy by Emmys. Workplace ensemble in a Chicago restaurant. Tonally heavier than your other loves; flagging because S2 episode 'Forks' and S3 'Fishes' are TV-history-grade.
items.pitch: Fine-dining chef returns home to take over his late brother's beef sandwich shop in Chicago.

critics.quote: Multiple Outstanding Comedy Emmys despite ongoing 'is it actually a comedy' debate.

items.title: Black Books
items.whyPriority: Dylan Moran's misanthropic London bookshop owner. Linehan-written. Three perfect short seasons. If you love Withnail-style British misery-comedy this is the TV match.
items.pitch: Bernard Black runs a bookshop he hates. Manny works for him. Fran is their friend. The shop is rarely open.

critics.quote: Won the BAFTA for Best Situation Comedy. Cult devotion in the UK and abroad.

items.title: Spaced
items.whyPriority: Edgar Wright's first directing project. Simon Pegg, Nick Frost, Jessica Hynes. Genre-pastiche-driven; the structural ancestor of Community.
items.pitch: Two London twentysomethings pretend to be a couple to rent a cheap flat. Each episode parodies a different film genre.

critics.quote: Established Wright/Pegg/Frost as a unit. Direct lineage to Hot Fuzz, Shaun of the Dead, Cornetto Trilogy.

sections.name: VI. Worth Considering
sections.desc: Less certain fits, but worth flagging.

items.title: Fleabag
items.pitch: Phoebe Waller-Bridge breaks the fourth wall to confide in the camera. Black comedy that turns devastating. Two short seasons that cohere as one work.

critics.quote: S2 swept 2019 Emmys. Considered one of the great comedy seasons of the streaming era.

items.title: The Larry Sanders Show
items.pitch: Late-night talk show host's offstage neuroses. The structural template for half of modern dark comedy (30 Rock, Curb, Office, Veep all owe it). HBO original.

critics.quote: Routinely cited as one of the most influential comedies in TV history.

items.title: Frasier (original)
items.dir: David Angell, Peter Casey, David Lee
items.pitch: Cheers spinoff. Seattle radio psychiatrist + brother + father + dog. Sharp, literary, multi-camera workplace-and-family ensemble.

critics.quote: Won 37 Emmys including five consecutive Outstanding Comedy Series — a record only recently approached.

items.title: Episodes
items.pitch: British sitcom writers are imported to LA to remake their show. Matt LeBlanc plays a heightened version of himself.

critics.quote: LeBlanc won the Globe; show was overlooked. Cult fans defend it strongly.

items.title: Will & Grace
items.pitch: Network sitcom about a gay lawyer and his straight female best friend, with Karen and Jack as scene-stealers. Cultural impact during early-2000s LGBTQ+ visibility shift was real.
items.whyPriority: You have watched 6+ episodes per Plex history. Cataloged for tracking.
